steps.title: Identify what 'increasing' means on the graph
steps.content: The height $y$ is increasing on an interval when the graph rises (positive slope) over that interval. For a launched object, height rises until it reaches its peak, then falls.

steps.title: Test the intervals
steps.content:
From $x=0$ to $x=2$, the graph rises continuously to its peak — so $y$ is increasing throughout.

From $x=0$ to $x=4$ includes the descent after the peak, so it isn't increasing the entire interval. From $x=2$ to $x=3$ and from $x=3$ to $x=4$ both fall on the descending side of the curve.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
